examples.title: OpenMP Parallel Loop (Data Parallelism)
examples.description: Example of data-parallel programming using OpenMP to parallelize a loop
examples.explanation: This OpenMP code demonstrates data parallelism: the same operation (matrix addition) is applied to different data elements (matrix rows/elements) concurrently. The #pragma omp parallel for directive distributes iterations across available threads. The schedule(static) indicates static load balancing - each thread gets equal chunks.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n²/p) where p = number of threads
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n²) for matrices, O(1) additional per thread

examples.title: MPI Point-to-Point Communication (Distributed Memory)
examples.description: Message passing between processes in a distributed memory system
examples.explanation: This MPI code demonstrates distributed memory programming. Each process has its own local memory and communicates via explicit messages. MPI_Send/MPI_Recv handle point-to-point communication. The code follows SPMD model - same executable runs on all processes with different data (rank determines behavior).
examples.timeComplexity: O(n/p + p × L) where L = latency per message
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n/p) local data per process + O(1) message buffers

examples.title: Task Parallel Pipeline with OpenMP Sections
examples.description: Demonstrates task parallelism using pipeline stages
examples.explanation: This demonstrates task parallelism - different functions executed concurrently on different threads. Unlike data parallelism (same operation on different data), task parallelism assigns different operations to different processors. Pipeline stages process different chunks of data simultaneously, achieving throughput improvement.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) serial vs O(n/3 + pipeline_overhead) parallel
